description_long: This is a dark navy blue, single-propeller military airplane with a classic World War II design, featuring a long nose and a bubble canopy cockpit. The wings are straight and slightly angled, with white star insignias and red stripes on the wingtips, indicating U.S. military markings. The tail section is adorned with red and white horizontal stripes on the vertical stabilizer, and the horizontal stabilizers are straight and set low. The propeller at the front is yellow-tipped, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retracted. The fuselage is streamlined, with visible panel lines and a single engine mounted at the nose. The cockpit is positioned forward and appears to be enclosed with a clear canopy.
description: A dark blue, single-propeller military airplane with U.S. insignia, bubble canopy, and red-striped tail, featuring a streamlined WWII-era design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, bubble canopy, and wide wings with U.S. star insignia.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the long nose, cockpit placement, and red-striped vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and wing insignia.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the propeller, cockpit, and tail markings with red stripes.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral angle and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration; landing gear not visible, likely retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a bubble canopy.
